steps.thought:
[Observation]
The current screen is the Android home screen. Visible apps include Gmail, Photos, YouTube, Phone, Messages, Chrome, and Calendar. The Settings app is not visible on this main screen.

[Plan]
Step 1: Open the Settings app → | Step 2: Navigate to Network & internet settings | Step 3: Toggle Wi-Fi off, task complete

[Decision]
To turn off Wi-Fi, I need to access the system settings. Since the Settings app icon is not present on the current home screen page, I will use a system-level command to open the Settings app directly.

[Memory]
First action, no prior memory.

[Observation]
The current screen is the Settings main page. Visible options include "Network & internet" (which mentions Mobile, Wi-Fi, hotspot), "Connected devices", "Apps", "Notifications", "Battery", "Storage", and "Sound & vibration".

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open the Settings app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Network & internet settings → | Step 3: Toggle Wi-Fi off, task complete

[Decision]
According to the current step in the plan, I need to access the network settings where the Wi-Fi toggle is located. The "Network & internet" option is clearly visible at the top of the list and explicitly mentions "Wi-Fi" in its subtitle. I will tap on this option to proceed.

[Memory]
App opened successfully. Target is to turn off Wi-Fi; currently navigating through Settings -> Network & internet.

[Observation]
The current screen is the "Network & internet" settings page. Visible options include "Internet" (Networks available), "Calls & SMS", "SIMs", "Airplane mode" (currently off), "Hotspot & tethering" (Off), "Data Saver" (Off), and "VPN" (None). There is no direct Wi-Fi toggle switch on this screen; instead, Wi-Fi settings are grouped under "Internet".

[Reflection]
The previous plan assumed Step 3 would be a direct "Toggle Wi-Fi off", but the interface requires going deeper into the "Internet" menu first to find the Wi-Fi toggle.

[Replan]
Step 1: Open the Settings app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Network & internet settings ✓ | Step 3: Access Internet settings → | Step 4: Toggle Wi-Fi off, task complete

[Decision]
Based on the updated plan, I need to access the Internet settings to find the Wi-Fi toggle. The "Internet" option at the top of the list manages available networks, which includes Wi-Fi. I will tap on "Internet" to proceed.

[Memory]
Navigated to Settings -> Network & internet. Wi-Fi settings are nested under the "Internet" menu, not on the main Network & internet page.

[Observation]
The current screen is the "Internet" settings page. The "Wi-Fi" toggle switch is visible and is currently in the off position (greyed out and toggled to the left). The T-Mobile network is connected, and there are other options like "Network preferences", "Saved networks", and "Non-carrier data usage".

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open the Settings app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Network & internet settings ✓ | Step 3: Access Internet settings ✓ | Step 4: Toggle Wi-Fi off, task complete →

[Decision]
Based on the observation, the Wi-Fi toggle is already in the off state, which aligns with the goal of the current step in the plan ("Toggle Wi-Fi off"). Since the objective "Turn wifi off" has already been met, no further action is required to change the state. I will declare the task complete.

[Memory]
Navigated to Settings -> Network & internet -> Internet. Wi-Fi toggle is confirmed to be in the off state. Task objective achieved.
